title: 10 possible side effects of Tanacetum parthenium (Feverfew) if used improperly
intro: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, is a herbal supplement that can have various health benefits when used properly, but it can also cause several side effects if misused. One potential side effect of improper use is mouth ulcers or stomach upset, as the herb contains compounds that can irritate the digestive tract. In addition, feverfew can increase the risk of bleeding, so it may interact with blood-thinning medications and lead to excessive bleeding. Prolonged use or high doses of feverfew can also cause muscle cramps, joint stiffness, and skin rashes. Furthermore, pregnant women and children should avoid feverfew, as it can lead to complications such as miscarriage or allergic reactions. Therefore, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking feverfew or any other herbal supplement.
